items.title: Linked-worktree gate and private runtime manifest
items.precondition: Run from the adapter worktree and separately from the primary Alloro checkout.
steps: Run the adapter from the primary checkout and confirm it refuses before startup.
Start a runtime from a verified secondary linked worktree.
Inspect the emitted manifest, permissions, safety fields, origin, ports, and stop command.
items.expected: Primary checkout is refused; the linked worktree emits a mode-0600, secret-free manifest tied to its path and HEAD.
items.evidence: Primary invocation returned: Test Worktree refused. Final manifests for runtimes isolated-test-worktree-adapt-f9de255b6a54 and isolated-test-worktree-adapt-d32ab89350a8 were mode 0600 and contained no JWT, password, API-key, secret, refresh-token, or access-token fields.
items.notes: Fresh -tw rerun on 2026-07-17: runtime isolated-test-worktree-adapt-e9ab4d9cb0f0 was tied to the exact linked-worktree path and HEAD, reported six distinct OS-assigned ports, and passed the local database health check with all fail-closed safety modes enabled.

items.title: Disposable database, Redis, schema, and fixture isolation
steps: Start baseline and gbp-posts runtimes concurrently.
Verify each runtime has distinct Postgres and Redis ports and containers.
Query synthetic row counts after the adapter seeds each fixture twice.
items.expected: Each runtime owns separate local dependencies and double-seeding leaves deterministic row counts.
items.evidence: Final same-source run: baseline counts users=2, organizations=1, organization_users=1, google_connections=0, work_items=0; gbp-posts counts 2,1,1,1,1. Explicit peer-worktree run reported pathsDistinct=true, originsDistinct=true, portsDistinct=true, runtimeIdsDistinct=true.
items.notes: Committed schema is structure-only, generated from alloro-dev PostgreSQL 17.9, and verified to exclude data statements, owners, grants, email-like values, and secrets.

items.title: One-time authenticated admin and Pilot bootstrap
items.precondition: Start the baseline runtime and open its authenticatedBootstrapUrl in an isolated browser context.
steps: Confirm redirect to /admin and that auth exists only in the unique origin localStorage.
items.expected: Admin opens without manual login, Pilot enters synthetic One Endodontics, no auth cookie or token URL is used, and bootstrap reuse returns 410.
items.evidence: evidence/final-baseline-pilot.png; bootstrap=200, localStorage token present, auth cookie absent, Pilot iframe title=Pilot session for worktree-client@example.test, synthetic organization visible, second bootstrap=410.
items.notes: Fresh -tw rerun on 2026-07-17: the tokenless bootstrap redirected to /admin, the private token file was consumed, reuse returned 410, and the embedded Pilot rendered the synthetic One Endodontics dashboard for Worktree Client. A non-blocking existing product error was also observed: SetupProgressContext attempted PUT /api/onboarding/setup-progress without an organization ID while the Pilot UI remained usable.

items.title: Local email and AI captures with outbound-write blocking
items.precondition: Start the baseline and gbp-posts fixtures.
steps: POST a synthetic email to the manifest email-capture port.
Generate a GBP local-post draft with a local synthetic image URL.
Attempt a non-local server-side POST through the outbound guard.
items.expected: Email and Anthropic requests are captured locally, the GBP draft is deterministic, and the non-local POST is blocked before network access.
items.evidence: Email capture JSONL recorded 1 synthetic message; Anthropic capture JSONL recorded the local fixture call; GBP endpoint returned 201 with safety_status=safe and evidence/final-gbp-posts.png shows Drafts 1; outbound attempt returned ALLORO_WORKTREE_OUTBOUND_BLOCKED with destination-only redacted logging.
items.notes: The original product concern was independently reproduced: an image-less post draft fails with featured_image_required. That product-flow fix is outside this infrastructure plan.

items.title: Named-worker allowlist and recurring-schedule suppression
steps: Scan the isolated Redis instance for repeat schedule keys.
Run the invalid-worker CLI case and the registry parity tests.
items.expected: Only gbp-automation starts, recurringScheduleCount is zero, no repeat keys exist, and unknown workers fail without leaving runtime artifacts.
items.evidence: worker-ready.json listed only gbp-automation and recurringScheduleCount=0; Redis repeat-key count=0; invalid worker exited 1 with runtime_dirs=0; targeted adapter/worker/email suite passed 42/42.

items.title: Full verification and clean teardown
steps: Run strict constitution checks, check:all, backend and frontend builds, TypeScript checks, targeted tests, and the full Vitest suite.
Stop both concurrent runtimes using their manifest stop commands.
Confirm no adapter containers, processes, runtime directories, or temporary peer worktree remain.
items.expected: All required gates pass and teardown removes only adapter-owned resources.
items.evidence: Strict conventions: 0 structural violations. check:all: exit 0. Backend build: pass. Frontend build: pass with existing bundle warnings. Root and adapter TypeScript: pass. Targeted tests: 42/42. Full suite: 542/542. Both final stop commands returned stopped; no alloro-tw containers/processes/runtime directories remained; disposable peer worktree was removed.
items.notes: check:all retained existing advisory totals: 438 lint warnings and 34 dependency warnings, with zero errors. Fresh -tw rerun on 2026-07-17: check:all exited 0, the targeted adapter suite passed 42/42, the manifest stop command returned stopped, all runtime PIDs exited, all labeled containers were removed, the private runtime directory was removed, and git diff --check passed.
